mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, mechanically depressing equity valuations and generating negative forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are not significantly lower when FEDFUNDS increases.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.5%, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending power, which reduces corporate profit expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60d returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE crosses the 5.5% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in real personal income and housing starts indicates a synchronized contraction in consumer demand and construction activity, which compresses earnings growth and produces negative forward S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The joint decline of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative returns, or single-series effects dominate the joint signal in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of monetary tightening, an accelerating industrial production index signals robust supply-side capacity utilization that outpaces demand constraints, supporting corporate margins and generating positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 5d returns during tightening periods, or the signal reverses direction when inflation expectations rise above 3%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising wage bills, which correlates with higher corporate revenue growth and generates positive forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no statistical relationship with 20d forward returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 260, it indicates entrenched inflationary pressures that force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, increasing equity risk premiums and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL levels above 260 do not precede negative 60d returns, or the threshold effect disappears after controlling for Fed policy expectations.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in real GDP and real personal income confirms broad-based economic growth that supports corporate earnings revisions, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive growth signal fails to predict positive returns, or GDPC1 and RSAFS diverge without affecting equity performance.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in the industrial production index signal a manufacturing contraction that reduces business investment and corporate profit forecasts, generating negative short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Consecutive INDPRO declines do not precede negative 5d returns, or the signal is overwhelmed by monetary policy announcements.
